description: The SACRED 8-Questions gate for a Trevor Otts Signature Presentation, plus the frame-selection question. DOCTRINE (Trevor's ruling -- one-question-at-a-time wins): the owner CONVERSATION is choice-first (offer a QUICK vs IN-DEPTH interview) and then asks ONE question at a time, never a wall of questions (AF-INTAKE-BATCH). The eight answers plus the chosen frame are then assembled into ONE atomic machine RECORD (working/copy/sp_intake.json) -- that assembled record is what prove_sp_intake.py validates (record_committed_atomically / mode=one_block describe the RECORD ledger being written as one atomic commit, NOT a batch of questions dumped at the owner). Prime Directive 8 is unchanged: once every answer is locked, all slides are written at one time. This file is the authoritative spec that prove_sp_intake.py loads.

delivery._alias_note: asked_all_at_once is a DEPRECATED alias for record_committed_atomically, kept for one release (fleet-ordering safety). one_question_per_turn was REMOVED from the record layer: it describes the conversation (which is one-per-turn), never the record commit, and prove_sp_intake.py no longer checks it.
delivery.rule: TWO LAYERS, kept distinct so they never conflict -- the 8 Questions are ASKED one at a time and RECORDED as one atomic ledger. (1) CONVERSATION LAYER (Trevor's ruling -- one-question-at-a-time wins): the front-door agent FIRST offers the quick-vs-in-depth CHOICE, then asks exactly ONE question at a time and waits for the owner's answer before sending the next -- never a batch. Dumping two or more of the 8 Questions in a single turn, or opening with no quick-vs-in-depth choice, is AF-INTAKE-BATCH (intake-conversation scoped; enforced_by qc_check -- 51-signature-presentation/scripts/intake_trace_check.py, a real deterministic transcript scanner (not just documented prose); it NEVER inspects, runs inside, or gates build_deck.py / run_signature_deck.py). (2) RECORD LAYER: once the answers are gathered one at a time, they are committed as ONE atomic intake ledger -- record_committed_atomically=true / mode=one_block describe THAT assembled record being written as a single atomic commit, which prove_sp_intake.py validates before slide authoring unlocks. These record fields are NOT a licence to dump the 8 Questions at the owner.

conversation_contract.choice_question: Love this -- let's shape it together and you set the pace. I can do this two ways: a QUICK interview (about 5 key questions, one at a time) or a more IN-DEPTH one (we go a little deeper, still one question at a time). Which would you like, quick or in-depth? You can switch or stop whenever you want.
conversation_contract.banned_batch_anti_pattern: A single message that enumerates the 8 Questions (title + alternate titles + avatar pain + story + teaching topic + teaching titles + offer + anything-else) together, or the screenshot phrase 'give me whatever you have got and I will get moving.' That message offers no choice and dumps the whole batch at once -- the canonical AF-INTAKE-BATCH failure.

delivery.front_door: brainstorming-buddy-presentations (signature mode) via deck-intake-driver.py signature mode

questions.prompt: What is the title of your Signature Presentation?
Do you want me to provide other possible titles before I start writing?
Any specific pain points to address in the avatar section?
Key elements of your story to consider before crafting the personal-story section?
What do you want to teach in the transformational-teaching section? (e.g., "7 Secrets to ___", "5 Ways to ___", "Mastering the ___ Protocol", "The ___ Blueprint to ___")
Do you want other possible titles for the transformational-teaching section?
What product(s) will you offer at the end?
questions.note: The EXACT product/offer name(s) captured here become the offer-token ledger. They are FORBIDDEN in Phase 3 (prove_sp_no_pitch.py) and REQUIRED to appear in the Phase-4 pitch. A named product here that is absent from the pitch-token ledger fails AF-SP-OFFER-UNDECLARED.

questions.prompt: Anything else to consider before I start writing?

frame_selection_question.prompt: Which Signature frame should carry your Transformational Teaching? (A) The Rulebook — you teach through numbered, non-negotiable Rules, each with an affirmation and a 3-step action plan, capped with a recap and a teased bonus Rule. (B) The Vault — you unlock numbered Secrets, each paired with a famous quote and its own affirmation, tied together by one running metaphor. (C) The Quest — you hand them a named Blueprint organized as Quests with steps and affirmations, closing on a poetic manifesto they finish in their own words. (D) The Original — we design a from-scratch frame around your own methodology. Not sure? Say 'show me' and I'll sketch your teaching topic in all three frames (one line each) before you pick.
frame_selection_question.orthogonal_to: STYLE_SOURCE (the visual match/analyze/create branch) — the frame governs the NARRATIVE, not the visual style; a signature deck still answers the visual branch and still gets the 3-variant style preview.

runtime_intake_contract.note: Shape of working/copy/sp_intake.json that prove_sp_intake.py validates. Any violation is exit 2, fail-closed.

required_fields.record_committed_atomically: must be true -- a RECORD-integrity field: the assembled intake ledger was written as ONE atomic commit after the one-at-a-time conversation (else AF-SP-8Q-SPLIT). It is NOT a licence to dump the 8 Questions at the owner; the CONVERSATION is choice-first and one question at a time (see delivery.conversation_contract / AF-INTAKE-BATCH). Deprecated alias: asked_all_at_once (accepted for one release).
required_fields.record_commit_ids: single non-empty id for the atomic intake-record commit (else AF-SP-8Q-SPLIT). Deprecated alias: question_block_msg_id (accepted for one release).
required_fields.answers: object carrying non-empty q1..q8 (any missing/empty → AF-SP-8Q-MISSING)
required_fields.offer_token_ledger: array of the exact product/offer names from q7 (must be non-empty; each must also be present in the Phase-4 pitch tokens or AF-SP-OFFER-UNDECLARED)

deprecated_aliases.asked_all_at_once: -> record_committed_atomically (accepted for one release; the machine layer no longer teaches batching)
deprecated_aliases.one_question_per_turn: REMOVED from the record layer -- it describes the (one-per-turn) conversation, never the record commit; prove_sp_intake.py no longer checks it

optional_fields.client_overrode_slide_floor: boolean — set true ONLY when the client states an exact slide count that overrides the ≥100 floor; consumed by prove_sp_structure.py and surfaced on the process certificate
optional_fields.client_exact_slide_count: integer — the client's explicit count when client_overrode_slide_floor is true

autofail_codes.AF-SP-8Q-MISSING: any of q1..q8 missing or empty (Directive 6)
autofail_codes.AF-SP-8Q-SPLIT: RECORD layer (deterministic, prove_sp_intake.py): the assembled intake ledger was not committed as ONE atomic ledger write -- record_committed_atomically!=true (deprecated alias asked_all_at_once!=true), or more than one record-commit id. Record-only gate; the conversation stays one question per turn. one_question_per_turn is NOT checked. This gates the machine RECORD, not the conversation.
autofail_codes.AF-INTAKE-BATCH: CONVERSATION layer (enforced_by qc_check -- 51-signature-presentation/scripts/intake_trace_check.py, the QC/Healer intake-trace scan; NEVER gates build_deck.py / run_signature_deck.py): the intake conversation dumped a batch -- two or more of the 8 Questions in a single assistant turn before the owner answered, OR it opened with no quick-vs-in-depth CHOICE first. Doctrine: delivery.conversation_contract. The banned reference anti-pattern is one message enumerating all 8 Questions together with 'give me whatever you have got and I will get moving.'
autofail_codes.AF-SP-FRAME-UNSET: signature_frame not one of the four allowed values (frame gate)
autofail_codes.AF-SP-TYPE-MISMATCH: deck_type != signature_presentation vs the run manifest
autofail_codes.AF-SP-OFFER-UNDECLARED: a product named in q7 is absent from the pitch-token ledger
